titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the Samuel Adams Green Papers
titlestmt.author: by Beinecke staff

publicationstmt.publisher: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

maintenanceagency.agencyname: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Tuesday June 18, 2019 at 03:07

corpname.part: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The Samuel Adams Green papers consist of correspondence, photographs, printed materials, audiocassette recordings, artwork, and other papers by or relating to curator, socialite, and celebrity assistant Samuel Adams Green. The bulk of the archive is organized into binders that document with correspondence, photographs, clippings, and other papers Green's relationships with notable figures including Cecil Beaton, Greta Garbo, John Lennon, Yoko Ono, and many other artists and celebrities. The collection also includes correspondence, photographs, clippings, and other papers documenting Green's work as an art curator and preservationist, including the 1965 Andy Warhol retrospective he organized while director of the Institute of Contemporary Art in Philadelphia, Pennsylvania. Additional personal papers include journals, day planners, a guest book from Green's home in Cartagena, Colombia, correspondence, photographs, and clippings. Additional photographs include slides from Green's travels. Audiovisual materials include audiocassettes containing demo recordings from Yoko Ono and John Lennon, as well as answering machine recordings of Andy Warhol, Cecil Beaton, and others.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: This collection is open for research.
Box 42 (audiovisual materials): Restricted fragile material. Reference copies may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.
Box 43 (digital media): Restricted fragile material. Access copies of digital files may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.

bioghist.head: Samuel Adams Green
bioghist.p: Samuel Adams Green (1940-2011) was an American art curator closely associated with the pop art movement, especially Andy Warhol, whose first retrospective was hosted by Green at the Institute for Contemporary Art, Philadelphia, in 1965. Green's active role in the art world ended by 1970, after which time he cultivated a career as a socialite, befriending and occasionally serving as an assistant to celebrities including Cecil Beaton, Greta Garbo, John Lennon, and Yoko Ono.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Purchased from Glenn Horowitz Bookseller, Inc. on the Edwin J. Beinecke Book Fund, 2012.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Samuel Adams Green Papers. General Collection, Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Collections are processed to a variety of levels, depending on the work necessary to make them usable, their perceived research value, the availability of staff, competing priorities, and whether or not further accruals are expected. The library attempts to provide a basic level of preservation and access for all collections as they are acquired, and does more extensive processing of higher priority collections as time and resources permit.
This collection received a basic level of processing, including rehousing and minimal organization.

Information included in the Description of Papers note and Collection Contents section is drawn from information supplied with the collection and from an initial survey of the contents. Folder titles appearing in the contents list below are often based on those provided by the creator or previous custodian. Titles have not been verified against the contents of the folders in all cases. Otherwise, folder titles are supplied by staff during initial processing.
This finding aid may be updated periodically to account for new acquisitions to the collection and/or revisions in arrangement and description.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: The Samuel Adams Green Papers is the physical property of the Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University. Literary rights, including copyright, belong to the authors or their legal heirs and assigns. For further information, consult the appropriate curator.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into five series: I. Binders, 1943-2009. II. Personal papers, 1960-2009. III. Photographs, 1970-1989. IV. Audiovisual materials, 1971-1993. V. Electonic media, undated.

corpname.part: University of Pennsylvania. Institute of Contemporary Art

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The Binders series consists of twenty-six binders that document with correspondence, photographs, clippings, and other papers Green's relationships with notable figures including Cecil Beaton, Greta Garbo, John Lennon, Yoko Ono, and many other artists and celebrities. The binders also includes correspondence, photographs, clippings, and other papers documenting Green's work as an art curator and preservationist, including the 1965 Andy Warhol retrospective he organized while director of the Institute of Contemporary Art in Philadelphia, Pennsylvania.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Also includes Eileen Hose correspondence.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Agnes Martin correspondence, related papers, and photographs

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Exhibition announcements for "Agnes Martin: New Paints" at the Pace Gallery, March 1-April 1 1975

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Institute of Contemporary Arts (ICA) records including photographs, clippings, correspondence, exhibition materials (including the Warhol retrospective), and other papers

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Institute of Contemporary Art (ICA) exhibition materials including Tony Smith, "7 Sculptors," "The Decisive Years," and "Art for the City"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Papers relating to independent projects and exhibitions including "The New Art: An Exhibition," Wesleyan University; Man on the Moon; "Scarves for Venice," "Sculpture in Environment," Jean Dubuffet's "Milord la Charmarre," Arman Project for Central Park, "American Painting of the 1960s" exhibition, and Susan Boettger.

did.unittitle: Binder #8 "People/Celebrities: Peter Allen -- Elizabeth of Yugoslavia"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Papers relating to Peter Allen, Steven Aronson, Ala von Ausberg, Brigid Berlin, Glen Bernbaum, David Croland, Jackie Curtis, Candy Darling, Daniel and Jennifer Dietrich, and Elizabeth of Yugoslavia

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Papers relating to Cyrinda Fox, C.Z. Guest, Rosemary Goldsmid, Jane Holzer, Carol Lebworth, Karen Lerner, Gladys (Gere) Marvel, Mary McFadden, Yvette Mimeux, Barry Paris, Naveen Patnaik, Cecilia Peck, Paloma Picasso, Christina Oxenburg/Damian Elwes, Alexis de Rede, and Philip van Rensselaer

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Papers relating to Tony Richardson, Theo Roubanis, Anthony P. Russo, D.D. Ryan, Dieter Schidor, John Schlessinger, Warren Sonbert, Jean Stein, Jacques Tiffeau, Richard Turley, Hugo Vickers, Lady Anne Waldegrave, Richard Wilbur, George Weidenfeld, Bernice Winterstein, Joan Worth, Robert Venturi, amd Diana Vreeland

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Papers relating to Henry Plumer Mcllhenny, Sandra Payson, Emily & Burton Tremaine, Eleanor Biddle Lloyd. Also includes magazine clippings.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Papers relating to Peter Beard, Christo & Jeanne Claude, Chryssa, Duchamp, Peter Erskine, Fred Forrest, and Charles "Chuck" Ginnever

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Papers relating to Robert Indiana, Philip Johnson, Gerard Malanga, Marisol, Robert Morris, Barnet Newman, Stephanie Gordon/Kenneth Noland, and Glen Orr

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Papers relating to Charles Ross, David von Schlegel, David Smith, Robert Stankiewcz, Elaine Sturtevant, Paul Thek, Jean Tinguely / Niki de Saint Phalle, May Wilson/Ann Wilson, and Robert Zakanych

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Papers including post-ICA job application letters, art consultant correspondence, and project proposals

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Papers relating to various projects and travel

did.unittitle: Binder #18 "Easter Island / Blue Cross Blue Shield Consultancy"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Photographs of Green's house in Cartagena and magazine clippings

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Greta Garbo newspaper clippings and two photographs

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Greta Garbo correspondence, photographs, and clippings

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Newspaper and magazine clippings

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Personal papers, corresondence, and photographs

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: John Lennon and Yoko Ono papers including correspondence, photographs, drawings by Sean Lennon, material relating to Apple records, and records of art purchases

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Photographs

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Photographs of Green's house in Cartagena

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Personal papers consist of journals, day planners, a guest book from Green's home in Cartagena, Colombia, correspondence, photographs, and clippings.

did.unittitle: Magazine and newspaper clippings, photographs, and correspondence
Artwork, guest book from Green's house in Cartagena, framed broadside

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Correspondents include Kerry Kennedy, Billy Holiday, Rajeev Sethi, Sylvia Miles, Donna Sheldon, Joanna Miles, John Samuals, New Art, Richard Artschwager, Peter Beard, and Leith Johnson. Also includes photographs.

did.unittitle: Photographs, negatives, interview material, Nancy Vanderberg correspondence and mail art, Sam Green publishing records, and other professional papers
Day planners, journals, writings, correspondence, interview transcript, and passports
Newspaper and magazine clippings related to John Lennon
Newspaper and magazine clippings related to John Lennon

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Photographs consist of slides from Green's travels to Greece, India, Egypt, France, Mexico, Guatemala, Fire Island, and elsewhere.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes slides labelled as follows: "India 1973," "Mexico Guatemala 1974 II," "S.A.G. Kashmir and Laddah," "Greece," "India," and "Dugal."

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes unlabelled slides and slides labelled as follows: "Greece 1970," "France 1972," "Corsica 1973?," "Egypt 1," "Egypt 2," and "Egypt 3."

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Restricted fragile. Reference copies may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes Yoko Ono and John Lennon demo recordings as well as recordings of Jackie Curtis, Cecil Beaton, Andy Warhol, Tom Dillon, and others, many of which are from Green's answering machine.

did.unittitle: The Jungman Sisters, Anne Wilson / Andy Warhol / H. / Beaton
did.materialspec: The program ends abruptly.

did.unittitle: O'K's songs to me, [ucc?] by piano only
did.materialspec: The program ends abruptly.
This audiocassette was found to be blank. No digitized files were created.
There is static on the tape.
The program begins abruptly. There is a high amount of hum on the tape.
The audio is distorted at times. The program ends abruptly.
There is a high amount of hum on the tape.

did.unittitle: Amel Nelson / Lallie Liola / Fred Hughes / Virginia / Beaton / Tremaine / Sta[lmyski]
did.materialspec: There is static on the tape. There is a high amount of hum and background noise on the tape. The program ends abruptly.
There is static on the tape.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: There is music throughout this cassette.

did.materialspec: There is a high amount of hum on the tape.
The program ends abruptly.

did.unittitle: Davidson Dinner / Sarah and Theo, Diamonds Short / Cecil Beaton, St. Regis
did.materialspec: There is a high level of background noise on the tape. The program ends abruptly.
This audiocassette was found to be blank. No digitized files were created.

did.unittitle: Hot Jaston 1st recording of "Double Fantasy", "Kiss Kiss" and "Starting Over"...
did.materialspec: There is static and a high amount of hiss on the tape. The program ends abruptly.
There is static on the tape. The left channel is recorded at a low level for a few minutes at about 46:30. The program ends abruptly.
There is a high amount of buzz and hum on the tape. The right channel is recorded at a lower level. The program is distorted at times. The recording ends abruptly.
There is a high amount of buzz on the tape. The program is distorted at times and ends abruptly.
There is static and a high amount of hum and buzz on the tape.

did.unittitle: Marie Rothschild / Garbo / Vanessa Redgrave / Garbo about photos
did.materialspec: There is static on the tape. The program ends abruptly.
There is a high amount of hum and buzz on the tape. The program ends abruptly.
There is a high amount of hum and background noise on the tape.
There is a high amount of buzz on the tape.

did.unittitle: Miss Brown / Woman of Ireland, Tom Dillon
did.materialspec: There is a high amount of buzz for the first 12 minutes. There is static on the tape. The program is distorted at times and ends abruptly.

did.unittitle: Side #5 Andy on art, Beaton / Side #6 Beaton, Andy
did.materialspec: There is a high amount of hum on the tape.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Restricted fragile material. Access copies of digital files may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.
